disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No GI alarm features — no dysphagia/odynophagia, no unintentional weight loss, no haematemesis/coffee-ground vomit, no melaena, no anaemia symptoms, no persistent vomiting, no epigastric mass
trigger_question_examples: Any trouble/pain swallowing, weight loss, vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, or black tarry stools?
Any persistent vomiting or a lump you can feel in the upper tummy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No swallowing trouble, no weight loss, no blood or black stools, and no persistent vomiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No cardiac features — not exertional, no radiation to arm/jaw, no diaphoresis/dyspnoea; strictly meal-related and reproducible on epigastric palpation
trigger_question_examples: Is it ever brought on by physical exertion, or does it spread to your arm/jaw with sweating or breathlessness?
Is it purely tied to eating?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's tied to eating, not exercise; no arm/jaw pain, no sweating or breathlessness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No NSAID use; age <60 without alarm features (H. pylori test-and-treat / empirical PPI appropriate)
trigger_question_examples: Do you take any anti-inflammatory painkillers like ibuprofen or naproxen regularly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't take anti-inflammatories or aspirin regularly.
